deadlines.action: SB 54 signed into law (Chapter 75, Statutes of 2022).
Producer Responsibility Advisory Board members appointed.
Statutory deadline to form and join a PRO.
Circular Action Alliance (CAA) selected as the state's sole, inaugural PRO.
Covered Material Categories list first published (updated Dec 2024).
Notice on EPS food service ware recycling-rate demonstration.
Plastic source-reduction baseline + Technical Report published (satisfies the ~1/1/2025 baseline).
EPS food service ware must demonstrate ≥25% recycling rate or be prohibited from sale.
Gov. Newsom directs CalRecycle to restart the SB 54 rulemaking (cost/clarity concerns).
Draft regulations withdrawn from OAL; revised draft + 15-day comment period (1/29–2/13/2026).
PERMANENT REGULATIONS APPROVED & EFFECTIVE (Title 14 CCR, Div. 7, Ch. 11.1). PEPRS portal launched; Producer Guidance site + estimated fee-rate ranges published.
2026 CA Producer Report (CY2025 data) + 2026 CA Annual Source Reduction Report (CY2025 data) due to CAA.
REGISTRATION DEADLINE (30 days from 5/1; Sun→Mon): (a) join CAA + submit 2023 baseline data; (b) register w/ CalRecycle as Independent Producer; or (c) apply Small Producer Exemption.

NGO SUIT FILED (confirmed): NRDC + Californians Against Waste Fdn + Oceana petition CalRecycle in SF Superior Court — regs allegedly inconsistent with SB 54 (chemical recycling, exclusions, toxics-fee explanation, federal-conflict basis).
CONFIRMED: CAA submitted draft California Program Plan (~350 pp) to the advisory board; 2027 budget projected up to $1.87B (5-yr $9.35B–$17.2B). Public comment opens.

FEDERAL SUIT FILED (confirmed): Nebraska-led 17-state coalition + NAW v. Heller & CAA (E.D. Cal., No. 2:26-at-01047) — asks the court to BLOCK enforcement; Commerce Clause, 1st Amendment (speech + compelled association), unlawful delegation to a private PRO.
SB 54 Advisory Board meeting (CalEPA HQ, Sierra Hearing Room, Sacramento; 10a–4p); CAA presents the draft program plan.
2023 baseline producer report due (CAA compiles→CalRecycle; independents report directly to CalRecycle).
CAA informational webinar on the draft California Program Plan.
Oregon EPR merits trial (NAW v. Feldon), Portland — Oregon's law was the first state EPR law blocked by preliminary injunction (Feb 2026); the outcome may shape how courts treat the SB 54 challenges.
Individual Source Reduction Plans due (exact date still to be confirmed by CAA).
CAA California Program Plan public comment period closes.
'Early fees' invoiced, based on the 2026 supply report (CY2025 data) — not on the 2023 baseline report.
SB 343 'chasing-arrows' enforcement begins; SB 343 recyclability determinations feed SB 54 (challenge pending).
CAA submits the FINAL California Program Plan to CalRecycle (after incorporating public comment on the June draft).
CalRecycle updates the statewide source-reduction baseline using 2023 data.
FULL PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION. Programme fees begin. Sales-restriction enforcement: must be in an approved PRO plan (or compliant individually) to sell covered materials.
Source-reduction interim: ≥10% of plastic covered material reduced, incl. ≥2% via reuse/refill.
CalRecycle transmits $500M surcharge liability to CDTFA (annually thereafter).
Environmental mitigation surcharge due (annually). PRO begins remitting $500M/yr to the Plastic Pollution Mitigation Fund. Non-PRO producers pay surcharge set by CalRecycle.
Plastic covered material: 30% recycling rate. EPS food service ware: 30%.
40% recycling rate. Source reduction ≥20% (≥4% reuse/refill). EPS 50%. CalRecycle evaluates surcharge increase.
END-STATE: 100% covered material recyclable or compostable; 65% plastic recycling rate; 25% source reduction (weight AND plastic components); EPS 65%.
$500M surcharge / Plastic Pollution Mitigation Fund provisions repealed (sunset).

watch: STATUS AS OF 3 JULY 2026 — Obligations remain in effect. No court has issued a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction, or stay against SB 54 in California. The pending lawsuits do not, by themselves, suspend SB 54 compliance; producers must continue to register, report, and prepare for fees. (Holland & Knight; Mondaq, late June 2026.)

FEDERAL (industry/states) — State of Nebraska et al. v. Heller et al., No. 2:26-at-01047, E.D. Cal., filed 22 June 2026. Seventeen state AGs + NAW (sole business plaintiff) seek to BLOCK enforcement on Commerce Clause, Import-Export Clause, First Amendment (compelled association + content-based speech), Due Process, and unlawful-delegation grounds. Defendants: CalRecycle Director Zoe Heller and Circular Action Alliance. No injunctive relief granted to date.

STATE (environmental) — NRDC + Californians Against Waste Foundation + Oceana v. CalRecycle, SF Superior Court, filed 2 June 2026. Argues the final regulations are WEAKER than SB 54 requires (chemical/advanced recycling counting, exclusions, inadequately explained toxics fee, federal-conflict basis). Pressures the regs from the opposite direction.

OREGON precedent — NAW v. Feldon: NAW members already hold a preliminary injunction (Feb 2026, the first state EPR law blocked); merits trial 13-17 July 2026, Portland. A SECOND Oregon suit — a class action for non-NAW producers — was filed 25 June 2026 with substantially similar claims; its injunction request is undecided. A producer-favorable ruling would be cited in the California federal case.

COLORADO parallel — Industry challenge in Colorado state court (filed Mar 2026) raises similar due-process and nondelegation claims; CDPHE's motion to dismiss is set for a hearing in July 2026. Colorado obligations also remain in effect meanwhile.

SB 343 (companion labeling law) — First Amendment challenge by the Flexible Packaging Association + AF&PA; a preliminary-injunction hearing was held in early June 2026 but the court has NOT yet posted a written decision. SB 343 recyclability determinations feed SB 54, so the outcome directly affects what counts as 'recyclable' here. Enforcement set for 4 October 2026.

POSSIBLE TARGET SLIP — CAA's draft California Program Plan acknowledges that the regulatory delays may require adjustments, including POTENTIALLY POSTPONING the law's 10% source-reduction target otherwise due 1 January 2027. Watch the final plan (October 2026) for confirmation.
